title: Authority Docs
description: External-to-substrate authority documents that drive design + implementation decisions for this arc. Filed as substrate citations so a session can traverse from an authority doc to the decisions / concepts / dispatch-modes / friction-items it grounds. Body is summary + pointer; full content stays at file_path (which may be inside or outside this repo).

file_path.description: Absolute or repo-relative path to the doc file

authority_kind.description: Class of authority — user-story carries the most weight for what-to-build prioritization; perspective documents the lived experience of a class of consumer

summary.description: 1-3 sentence summary of what this doc establishes

key_claims.description: Atomic claims the doc makes that ground decisions

scope.description: What the doc is authoritative about (e.g. 'jit-agents tool surface UX'); bounds the doc's grounding power

surface_kills_mapping.description: Structured capture of 'kills FB-X' claims the authority document makes in prose. Each entry pins a single named surface to the friction items the doc claims it eliminates, with exact source file + line + verbatim claim text for audit. The implementing_feature_id links the authority claim to the planning unit responsible for realizing it; per-arc invariants compare authority's claimed kills against the implementing FEAT's actual planning edges and against verification-attested resolutions.

surface_name.description: Exact name of the surface as written in the authority doc (e.g. 'list-dispatch-models', 'author-agent-spec (fixed)')

source_file.description: Repo-relative or absolute path to the authority doc file the claim appears in (typically matches the parent doc's file_path)

source_line.description: Line number within source_file where the verbatim kills claim appears

claim_text.description: Verbatim text of the kills claim from the source (e.g. 'Kills FB-001, FB-002, FB-003, FB-005, FB-006.')

killed_friction_ids.description: Friction item IDs the surface claims to eliminate (parsed from claim_text)

implementing_feature_id.description: Optional. The FEAT in this substrate that implements this authority surface. When set, per-arc invariants assert that FEAT.feature_depends_on_item edges to FB items match killed_friction_ids exactly; on TASK completion + VER attestation, each FB flips to status=resolved and a verification_kills_friction_item edge from VER to FB is required.

oid.description: Content-independent substrate-stable item identity (content-addressed substrate identity, Cycle 3). Minted once at item birth via mintOid(substrate_id); immutable across content versions. Optional in the schema so pre-Cycle-3 items validate; stamped on next write.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 (hex) of the RFC-8785-canonical content projection of this item (metadata fields excluded). Recomputed on every stamping write; moves with content, stable across metadata-only churn.

content_parent.description: content_hash of the immediately-prior version of this item; set on a content-changing update, absent on the first (v1) version. Forms the per-item content version chain.
